prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of formaldehyde (H₂C=O) was computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (PySCF, tier 'standard', density-fitted RKS), in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Partial charges were computed via Mulliken scheme on the neutral (N), cation (N−1, doublet), and anion (N+1, doublet) states. The headline value reported is the maximum f⁺ index, which belongs to the carbon atom (atom 2): f⁺(C) = 0.70839, identifying it as the most electrophilic site. The most nucleophilic site is oxygen (atom 1): f⁻(O) = 0.46742. The dual descriptor for C is +0.59595, confirming dominant electrophilic character at the carbonyl carbon — chemically consistent with the well-known susceptibility of the carbonyl carbon to nucleophilic addition. All integrity checks passed (trustworthy: true); no warnings were issued; Fukui charge conservation sums to 1.0000 for both f⁺ and f⁻.
